content:
I built inup — an interactive CLI for upgrading outdated dependencies.
Run npx inup in any project and you get an arrow-key list of everything outdated, toggle what you want, hit enter, it writes and installs.
yarn upgrade-interactive was the inspiration — I always liked that flow, but most of my projects are pnpm, so I wanted the same thing without being locked into yarn. Ended up auto-detecting the package manager from the lockfile so it works the same everywhere, yarn included.
Also handles monorepo workspaces in one pass and flags known vulnerabilities inline. Headless mode available for CI, and there's a GitHub Action on the Marketplace too.
No telemetry, MIT.
Repo: https://github.com/donfear/inup

  submitted by   /u/mikeredson
[link]   [comments]
